outcome_contract.why: Guests with confirmed bookings arrive at the hostel with no bed assigned because staff cannot see unallocated occupants anywhere on the rooms grid. This is an operational gap: the data exists but is hidden from the staff view.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Staff can identify all unallocated occupants directly from the rooms-grid page before guests arrive, eliminating the failure mode where guests arrive with no room assignment.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `RoomsGrid.tsx` uses DS `Stack`/`Inline`/`Cluster`; per-room panels use `border-border-2`, `bg-surface`, `text-foreground` tokens; `StatusLegend.tsx` for colour key
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No unallocated section/panel in layout; new panel must match DS token usage and existing visual language
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Define visual placement (above rooms? below? distinct colour?) and component structure

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Room panels show loading spinner and error message at page level; no room-level empty state
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Empty unallocated panel (zero unallocated occupants) must not render cluttered UI; loading and error states must propagate
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Specify empty-state: hide panel entirely vs show "no unallocated bookings" message; define loading/error passthrough

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Internal tool; no external auth surface; reads existing Firebase subscription data already subscribed on page load
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No new auth surface introduced; data is already loaded — panel only reshapes existing data

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No current observability for dropped unallocated occupants (silent drop at line 241)
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Staff have no visibility today; unallocated occupants are invisible without this feature. No logging added at the drop point.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Consider adding a `console.warn` or telemetry event when unallocated occupants are detected — gives future observability seam

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `useGridData.test.ts` covers allocation-match path; no test for absent/null `allocated`
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Coverage gap for unallocated path in `useGridData`; no component test for new panel
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Write unit tests for computation + rendering as outlined in Test Landscape

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `IGuestByRoomData.allocated: string` typed non-optional but may be absent at runtime in Firebase snapshot; `useGridData` return type does not include unallocated surface
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Runtime type mismatch risk: `allocated` can be absent despite non-optional TypeScript type; new computed value must be typed correctly in return
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Add `unallocatedOccupants` to `useGridData` return type; verify `allocated` optionality in `IGuestByRoomData`

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: All four Firebase subscriptions are live (`onValue`) already subscribed; `useMemo` recomputes on any of 7 deps; current data size: 10 rooms × N bookings
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Adding unallocated computation adds one additional `useMemo` pass over the same `bookingsData` + `guestByRoomData` — O(N) additional scan, no extra Firebase reads; safe
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Verify N (active bookings count) is bounded; worst-case: large multi-week view with many unallocated occupants

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Next.js app deployed to Cloudflare Worker (`@opennextjs/cloudflare`); no feature flags in codebase
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: New component is additive; rollback is a revert of the new component + hook addition; no data migration
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Plan is purely additive — no rollback complexity; no feature flag needed for internal tool

open_operator_questions: When an occupant is shown in the unallocated panel, should staff be able to act on the allocation, and if so, what is the safe UX path?

stage_payload.scope_summary: The rooms-grid page (`/rooms-grid`) shows one `RoomGrid` per known room (rooms 3–12). Occupants that have a booking in Firebase but have not yet been assigned a bed appear in `guestByRoomData` with a missing, empty, or null `allocated` field. The filter `guestByRoomData[occId]?.allocated !== room` in `useGridData.ts` (line 241) silently drops these occupants — they never appear in any room panel, making them invisible to staff. The change being considered is adding a new "Unallocated" panel (or badge/section) to the rooms-grid page that collects and displays every occupant whose `guestByRoomDa
goals: Surface all unallocated occupants on the rooms-grid page within the selected date window.
Show enough context per occupant (name, booking ref, check-in/check-out date, occupant status) for staff to take action.
Keep the implementation additive — no changes to existing room panels or the filter logic that powers them.
Integrate cleanly with the existing `useGridData` data pipeline (Firebase live subscription).
non_goals: Adding a net-new bed-allocation workflow. Note: `BookingDetailsModal` already contains a "Move Booking" room-assignment action backed by `useAllocateRoom`; whether the unallocated panel exposes that existing workflow is an open scope question for analysis.
Adding server-side push notifications for unallocated bookings.
Changes to the Firebase data model or how `allocated` is written.
constraints: Internal tool — English only, no i18n required.
Must work with the existing Firebase Realtime Database subscription pattern (`onValue`).
`useRoomConfigs` returns a static list of known rooms (3–12). Occupants whose `allocated` value is not in `knownRooms` are also effectively unallocated from the grid's perspective.
No new Firebase paths — reads only existing `bookings`, `guestByRoom`, `guestsDetails`, `activities` nodes.
assumptions: An occupant is "unallocated" if `guestByRoomData[occId]?.allocated` is absent, `undefined`, `null`, or an empty string, OR if the value is not in the `knownRooms` list.
The date-range filter (checkInDate/checkOutDate vs page startDate/endDate) should apply to the unallocated panel as well — no value in showing occupants outside the selected window.
The `booked` field on `guestByRoomData` entries records the originally booked room and is informational; `allocated` is the actual assignment.
key_entry_points: `apps/reception/src/app/rooms-grid/page.tsx` — Next.js route page, dynamically imports `RoomsGridClient` with `ssr: false` to prevent Firebase initialisation during SSR
`apps/reception/src/app/rooms-grid/RoomsGridClient.tsx` — wraps `<Providers>` + `<RoomsGrid>`; this is the client boundary entry

analysis_readiness.Recommended next step: `/lp-do-analysis reception-roomgrid-unallocated-panel` — a separate hook duplicating the four Firebase subscriptions would contradict the additive goal. The implementation approach is to extend `useGridData`'s existing `useMemo` to compute and return `unallocatedOccupants`. Analysis should focus on: (A) computing within the existing `allRoomData` memo vs (B) a second `useMemo` in the same hook; and visual placement of the unallocated panel. Note: `BookingDetailsModal` reuse has been ruled out for v1 (unsafe — moves all occupants under a booking ref). Analysis should confirm operator read-only

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: [Type contract gap] [Minor]: `IGuestByRoomData.allocated` is typed `string` (non-optional) but Firebase may omit the key. Should be `allocated?: string`.
rehearsal_issues.Resolution Required: No (advisory; safe to address in build)

rehearsal_issues.Scope Area: Test landscape — existing mocked hook pattern reusability

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: [Scope gap] [Critical]: `handleConfirmMoveBooking` loops ALL occupants under the booking ref — using it from an occupant-level panel would move all roommates. Additionally requires `date` context not present in unallocated row. **Resolved by scoping v1 as read-only panel.**

process_topology.trigger: Staff opens the rooms-grid page in Reception.
process_topology.end_condition: Staff sees all allocated bookings in per-room panels; unallocated bookings are silently absent.

areas.label: Data loading
areas.flow: Page mounts → `useGridData` subscribes to Firebase `bookings`, `guestByRoom`, `guestsDetails`, `activities` nodes

areas.label: Occupant filtering
areas.flow: For each (bookingRef, occId) in `bookingsData`, check `guestByRoomData[occId]?.allocated !== room` — if not matching the current room, skip entirely
areas.issues: **Occupants without an `allocated` value are dropped here silently**

areas.label: Room panel rendering
areas.flow: `RoomsGrid.tsx` iterates `knownRooms` (rooms 3–12), renders one `RoomGrid` per room with filtered data

areas.label: Staff action
areas.flow: Staff can see allocated occupants and click cells to see booking details
areas.issues: No action possible for unallocated because they are invisible
